target.name: Alef Aeronautics
target.founder: Jim Dukhovny, Constantine Kisly, Pavel Markin, Oleg Petrov

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Alef Aeronautics is a Series A–stage transportation company that has raised $30M+ to date. The transportation sector contains eight private peers at various stages, including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger; no public comparables exist in a sufficiently narrow peer set.

The stage-progression method is applicable here. Using the $30M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the subsequent round yields an implied valuation of $60M. The peer-median method is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Transportation sector; while five same-sector peers exist, none of the filtered subsets—by stage or valuation tier—yielded three or more datapoints necessary for reliable median calculation. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unwarranted noise.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, with a median of $60M. The narrow range reflects dependence on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the absence of near-peer valuation data to validate the stage-progression assumption and limited visibility into Alef's unit economics, path to commercialization, and competitive positioning within advanced transportation. Further data on adjacent-stage or sector-comparable transaction multiples would strengthen conviction in this range.
